title: TEA Evidence Bundle
description: Portable validation package for one signed TEA object. A TEA Evidence Bundle binds together the signed object identity, the digital signature, signing certificate material, timestamp evidence, transparency evidence, and optional validation material needed for long-term and offline verification. This schema reflects the TEA Trust Architecture profile, where timestamps and transparency evidence are required.

bundleVersion.description: Version of the TEA Evidence Bundle format. This allows future schema evolution while keeping validation deterministic for a specific version.

object.description: Reference to the signed TEA object that this bundle validates. This does not embed the object itself; it identifies the object by type and digest.

signature.description: The digital signature over the exact bytes of the target object. The signature is part of the evidence bundle and is the primary cryptographic binding anchor for timestamps and transparency evidence.

certificate.description: The signing certificate containing the public key used to verify the signature.

timestamps.description: Timestamp evidence for the signature. In the TEA Trust Architecture, at least one timestamp is required. Timestamps are expected to bind to the signature or its canonical serialized representation and prove that the signature existed within the certificate validity window.

transparency.description: Transparency evidence entries. In the TEA Trust Architecture, at least one transparency entry is required, and at least one of them must come from Rekor or Sigsum. SCITT may be included as additional evidence but is not sufficient on its own.

validationMaterial.description: Optional supporting material included for portability, preservation, or offline validation convenience. This material does not replace the core evidence model.

algorithm.description: Digest algorithm identifier. TEA currently requires SHA-256 for evidence-bundle digests and bindings.

value.description: Digest value encoded using a deployment-defined representation, typically base64url or hexadecimal. The encoding must be used consistently by the implementation.

digest.description: A cryptographic digest object. In TEA evidence bundles this is the standard way to identify objects and bound representations.

objectType.description: Type of signed TEA object. Typical values include tea-collection, artifact, compliance-document, discovery-document, and dsse-attestation. This vocabulary may be constrained further by TEA profiles or implementation policy.

objectId.description: Optional implementation-specific identifier for the signed object. This is informative and must not be treated as the primary cryptographic identity.

mediaType.description: Optional media type of the signed object. This helps consumers interpret or retrieve the object but is not the trust anchor.

location.description: Optional URI from which the object may be retrieved. This is a convenience pointer and does not define trust.

objectRef.description: Reference to the signed object. The digest is the authoritative binding to the object bytes.

format.description: Signature container or representation format. The selected format determines how the signature value is interpreted and verified.

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm identifier. TEA currently requires Ed25519 for the TEA-native trust architecture profile.

value.description: Serialized signature value or serialized signature object in the declared format.

signatureDigest.description: Optional digest of the signature object. This is useful when timestamps and transparency evidence bind to the signature representation rather than directly to the raw signed object.

signature.description: The digital signature over the target object. This is the core cryptographic proof of origin and integrity.

certificate.description: The signing certificate used to validate the signature.

chain.description: Optional additional certificate material included for portability or offline verification support. This may be useful in some deployment models, but it does not replace TEA trust-anchor or evidence validation rules.

fingerprint.description: Optional SHA-256 fingerprint of the signing certificate.

certificate.description: Certificate object containing the signing certificate and optional supplementary certificate material.

subjectDN.description: Subject Distinguished Name from the TSA certificate. This is an informational representation of the TSA identity.

publicKeyFingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the TSA public key. This is the stable identifier of the TSA key used for timestamp signing.

timestampAuthorityRef.description: Reference to the TSA derived from the TSA certificate. The TSA certificate used to sign the timestamp token remains authoritative.

format.description: Timestamp format identifier. TEA currently uses RFC 3161 timestamps.

messageImprint.description: Optional message imprint extracted from the timestamp token. For RFC 3161, this should correspond to the SHA-256 digest of the signature or other canonical signed representation defined by TEA policy.

tsaCertificateChain.description: Optional TSA certificate chain included for portability and offline validation.

timestamp.description: Timestamp evidence for the signature. The TSA identity is derived from the TSA certificate used to sign the timestamp token. Any explicit TSA reference fields are informational and MUST NOT be treated as separate trust anchors.

type.description: What the transparency evidence is bound to. TEA policy generally prefers binding to the signature or timestamped-signature rather than merely to the raw object.

bindingRef.description: Explicit description of the object to which transparency evidence is bound.

transparencyVerification.description: System-specific transparency verification material. This intentionally remains flexible so the same bundle format can support Rekor, Sigsum, and future SCITT receipts.

integratedTime.description: Integration time or equivalent log-recorded time, if provided by the transparency system.

inclusionProof.description: System-specific inclusion proof or equivalent proof object.

signedTreeHead.description: Signed tree head or equivalent append-only checkpoint.

receipt.description: Receipt object or serialized receipt for systems such as SCITT.

logSignature.description: Optional log signature or signed statement used by the transparency system.

witnessSignatures.description: Optional witness signatures, relevant for systems such as Sigsum.

system.description: Transparency system identifier. Rekor and Sigsum are the current baseline systems for TEA Trust Architecture compliance. SCITT may appear as additional evidence.

evidenceType.description: System-specific evidence type, for example hashedrekord, dsse, log-entry, or receipt.

format.description: Serialization format of the trust anchor or verification key.

value.description: Serialized trust anchor or verification key material.

system.description: Optional system identifier to which this trust anchor applies, for example rekor, sigsum, scitt, or tsa.

trustAnchor.description: Optional trust anchor material included to support offline or portable verification.

certificateDigest.description: Optional digest of the signing certificate.

tsaTrustAnchors.description: Optional TSA trust anchors included for portability and offline validation.

transparencyVerificationKeys.description: Optional transparency-system verification keys or trust anchors.

notes.description: Optional implementation notes or preservation comments.

validationMaterial.description: Optional supplementary material for portability, preservation, or offline validation.

notes: logged to two independent transparency systems
